statements.titleEn: The municipality should take measures to reduce through car traffic on the inner-city ring road.

statements.title: At least 40% of the new homes to be built in the municipality must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.
statements.moreInfo: Social rental homes are intended for people with lower incomes. In ’s-Hertogenbosch the current policy is that at least 30% of new housing must be social rent. Of the existing homes just over 33% are social rental housing.
statements.pro: There is a large shortage of affordable housing. Low-income people thus get more opportunities for housing. Neighborhoods become more mixed with residents of lower and higher incomes.
statements.con: People with slightly higher incomes will find it harder to obtain housing. If mainly social rental homes and fewer higher-priced homes are added, it becomes harder for people to move to the next home. Applying the same standard to every new construction project leaves too little room for tailored solutions.
statements.titleEn: At least 40% of the new homes to be built in the municipality must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

statements.title: People who need care or support should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.
statements.moreInfo: For care and support from the municipality, such as domestic help or guidance, residents sometimes have a preference for a caregiver with a particular background. In the current care system the assignment of a caregiver does not take into account the cultural or religious background of the person receiving care.
statements.pro: Care should be person-centered. Self-direction is important in care and support. Care fits better when a person’s background is taken into account.
statements.con: Due to staff shortages, freedom of choice is not always feasible. Care is neutral: good care can also be provided without a shared background. Unlimited choice of any preferred provider is too expensive.
statements.titleEn: People who need care or support should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

statements.title: The municipality should contribute financially to the expansion of the animal shelter.
statements.moreInfo: The animal shelter in ’s-Hertogenbosch takes in dogs and cats that have been found or are wandering. Each year this concerns more than 500 animals. On average there are about 70 animals in the shelter at the same time. The shelter wants to expand, but there is not enough money.
statements.pro: Animal welfare is important. The municipality is responsible for the reception of found animals. The animal shelter does not have enough space to properly accommodate all animals.
statements.con: Expansion costs the municipality extra money. Information campaigns should reduce the need for shelter. Ongoing support can lead other organizations to expect funding from the municipality as well.
statements.titleEn: The municipality should contribute financially to the expansion of the animal shelter.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should spend more money on the maintenance of neighborhoods.
's-Hertogenbosch should hold citizens' assemblies more often to advise the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents selected by lot.
When allocating building land, companies that provide jobs in the municipality should be given priority.
In new neighborhood developments there should always be a place for music and cultural associations.
Residents should be able to have a say in the budget for their neighborhood or village.
The municipality should not impose additional rules on companies beyond national regulations.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.
Single-person households should pay less for the waste disposal charges.

shootoutStatements.theme: Widening the eligibility for poverty assistance
shootoutStatements.title: More low-income people should be able to make use of poverty support schemes.
The municipality should more strictly monitor abuses in the housing of migrant workers.
Permitted opening hours for restaurants and cafés should be extended.
The municipality should invest more in fast cycling routes.
The municipality should invest more in sports facilities.
When allocating a social rental home, applicants from the municipality should continue to be given priority wherever possible.
Each neighborhood should have permanent care professionals who provide long-term support to residents.
Dumping of waste should be tackled more strictly.
The municipality should provide shelters and emergency stockpiles for crisis situations.
The city center of 's‑Hertogenbosch should also remain accessible by car.
Local initiatives that increase contact between residents should receive extra subsidies.
Seniors who move to a smaller home should receive a moving allowance.
There should be an eastern access road for Rosmalen and the eastern part of the municipality.
The municipality should provide more funding to support informal caregivers.
The municipality should stop expanding welfare services.
The municipality should invest more money in meeting places for older people.
